text: AI is increasingly used in creative fields. Software can now generate realistic paintings in seconds, compose original music in various styles, and even write articles or poems. This AI-generated art raises an important question: is this true creativity? Human creativity often involves personal experience, emotion, and intention behind the work. Machines, however, rely on data and algorithms to create patterns. While AI can produce impressive results, it does not feel or understand what it makes. For many, the true value of art lies in the human connection and meaning behind it. As AI tools become more common, the debate between human creativity and machine creativity continues to evolve.
